passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: Philosophy often addresses questions that, by their very nature, are incapable of being answered through empirical observation alone.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text: Hardly had the new law been implemented when the public started protesting against its strict regulations.

questions.question: Rarely has a discovery in the field of linguistics sparked as much debate as the emergence of the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, which suggests that language dictates thought.

options.A: The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, claiming that language shapes our cognition, has caused more controversy than almost any other find in linguistics.
options.B: Most linguistic discoveries are less controversial than the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, even though it suggests that language and thought are separate.
options.C: Linguists rarely agree on the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis because it fails to explain the relationship between language and human thought.
options.D: The emergence of the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis is unique because it is the only linguistic discovery that has ever been debated.
options.E: That language determines thought is a common belief in linguistics, and the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis is the most famous example of this.

questions.question: Despite the significant advancements in renewable energy technologies, fossil fuels continue to dominate the global energy market due to their established infrastructure.

options.A: Fossil fuels are still the primary energy source globally because of existing systems, even though renewable energy tech has improved greatly.
options.B: Renewable energy will never replace fossil fuels because the infrastructure for green energy is too expensive to build.
options.C: Because fossil fuels have a better infrastructure, renewable energy technologies have not seen any significant advancements lately.
options.D: The global energy market prefers fossil fuels over renewable energy because they are more efficient and cheaper to produce.
options.E: Advancements in renewable energy have finally allowed it to compete with the infrastructure of the fossil fuel industry.

questions.question: Had the Roman Empire not expanded its borders so extensively, it might have managed its internal administrative crises more effectively.

options.A: The Roman Empire was able to handle its internal problems only because it expanded its borders to new territories.
options.B: The vast expansion of the Roman Empire likely hindered its ability to deal with its internal administrative issues.
options.C: Because the Roman Empire failed to expand, it suffered from numerous administrative crises that led to its downfall.
options.D: If the Romans had focused on administration instead of expansion, they would have been a much larger empire.
options.E: Administrative crises in Rome were caused by the lack of external expansion rather than the size of the empire.

questions.question: It is not so much the content of the message as the tone in which it is delivered that determines the audience's reaction.

options.A: The audience usually ignores the tone of a message if the content is interesting enough to hold their attention.
options.B: How a message is conveyed is more influential on the audience's response than the actual information it contains.
options.C: Both the content and the tone are equally important when trying to get a positive reaction from an audience.
options.D: If the tone of a message is inappropriate, the audience will never understand the underlying content.
options.E: The reaction of the audience is determined primarily by the facts presented rather than the speaker's delivery.

questions.question: While the benefits of artificial intelligence are undeniable, the ethical implications regarding privacy cannot be overlooked.

options.A: The advantages of AI are so great that we should ignore any potential privacy concerns they might cause.
options.B: AI provides many benefits; however, we must also consider the serious concerns it raises about personal privacy.
options.C: Privacy is more important than the benefits offered by AI, which is why its development should be stopped.
options.D: Because AI has undeniable benefits, it is impossible for it to have any negative ethical implications.
options.E: The ethical issues of AI are more widely discussed than the benefits it brings to the field of privacy.

questions.question: Only by implementing stringent regulations can the government hope to curb the rising levels of industrial pollution.

options.A: The government has already implemented strict rules, yet industrial pollution continues to rise steadily.
options.B: Strict regulations are the only way the government can expect to reduce the increase in industrial pollution.
options.C: Industrial pollution is rising because the government refuses to implement any kind of regulations.
options.D: If the government curbs pollution, it will then be able to implement more stringent industrial regulations.
options.E: The government's hope is to regulate industry so that they do not have to deal with pollution issues.

questions.question: The architectural style of the Renaissance was characterized by a revival of classical symmetry and proportion, moving away from Gothic complexity.

options.A: Renaissance architecture abandoned the intricate Gothic style in favor of returning to classical balance and ratios.
options.B: Gothic complexity was actually the foundation upon which Renaissance architects built their symmetrical designs.
options.C: The Renaissance style was a mix of Gothic elements and classical symmetry, creating a unique architectural blend.
options.D: Architects in the Renaissance preferred Gothic complexity over the simple symmetry found in classical buildings.
options.E: Because they disliked classical proportion, Renaissance architects developed a more complex Gothic style.

questions.question: In the absence of a reliable vaccine, the only effective strategy for controlling the virus was widespread social distancing.

options.A: Social distancing was more effective than a vaccine in controlling the spread of the virus during the pandemic.
options.B: Because there was no dependable vaccine, social distancing on a large scale was the sole successful method for virus control.
options.C: Even with a reliable vaccine, social distancing remains the best strategy to keep the virus from spreading.
options.D: The virus was controlled through social distancing because the available vaccines were not reliable enough.
options.E: Wide-scale social distancing was abandoned as soon as a reliable vaccine for the virus was finally developed.

questions.question: The decline of the Mayan civilization remains a subject of intense academic scrutiny, with theories ranging from drought to internal warfare.

options.A: Academics have finally agreed that drought and warfare were the primary causes of the Mayan collapse.
options.B: Scholars are still closely studying why the Mayans declined, proposing various causes like war or lack of rain.
options.C: Internal warfare was a more significant factor in the Mayan decline than the droughts suggested by some academics.
options.D: The Mayan civilization declined so rapidly that scholars have been unable to form any plausible theories.
options.E: Drought and internal warfare are the only two theories that explain the decline of the Mayan civilization.

questions.question: Cognitive psychology posits that our perception of reality is a mental construct rather than a direct reflection of the external world.

options.A: According to cognitive psychology, we see the world exactly as it is without any mental processing.
options.B: Cognitive psychology suggests that what we perceive as reality is built by our minds, not just a mirror of the outside world.
options.C: The external world is a mental construct that cognitive psychologists use to explain the nature of reality.
options.D: Our perception is often wrong because cognitive psychology cannot accurately reflect the external world.
options.E: Reality is a direct reflection of our thoughts, which is the main theory supported by cognitive psychologists.

questions.question: Although the author's prose is often dense and difficult to navigate, the profound insights it offers make the effort worthwhile.

options.A: The author's writing is so complex that most readers find it impossible to gain any useful insights from it.
options.B: Reading the author's work is rewarding because of its deep ideas, despite the challenging and heavy writing style.
options.C: The insights in the book are as difficult to understand as the author's dense and complicated prose.
options.D: If the author's prose were less dense, the insights offered in the book would be even more profound.
options.E: The effort required to read the book is not justified because the author's insights are not very deep.

questions.question: The sudden surge in inflation has forced the central bank to reconsider its plan for lowering interest rates this quarter.

options.A: The central bank will definitely lower interest rates this quarter despite the unexpected increase in inflation.
options.B: Because inflation rose sharply, the central bank's intention to reduce interest rates this quarter is being re-evaluated.
options.C: Lowering interest rates is the central bank's strategy to combat the sudden and unexpected surge in inflation.
options.D: Inflation has increased because the central bank failed to lower interest rates as they had previously planned.
options.E: The central bank has decided to increase interest rates instead of lowering them due to the surge in inflation.

questions.question: Species extinction is occurring at a rate unprecedented in human history, primarily due to habitat loss and climate change.

options.A: Habitat loss and climate change are causing species to die out faster than at any other time since humans have existed.
options.B: Throughout human history, climate change has always been the main reason for the extinction of various species.
options.C: The rate of species extinction was much higher in the past, before humans began to change the climate and destroy habitats.
options.D: If we could stop habitat loss, the rate of species extinction would return to what it was earlier in human history.
options.E: Humans have always caused species extinction, but climate change is a relatively new factor in this process.

questions.question: The success of the mission was contingent upon the seamless integration of various engineering subsystems.

options.A: The mission was successful because each engineering subsystem was developed independently of the others.
options.B: Whether the mission succeeded depended on how well the different engineering parts worked together as a whole.
options.C: The engineering subsystems were so well integrated that the success of the mission was guaranteed from the start.
options.D: Despite the lack of integration among the subsystems, the mission still managed to be a success.
options.E: The mission failed because the engineering subsystems could not be integrated seamlessly.

questions.question: Virtually no aspect of modern life remains untouched by the influence of digital technology.

options.A: Digital technology has had a minor impact on most areas of our lives today.
options.B: There is almost no part of contemporary existence that hasn't been affected by digital tech.
options.C: Modern life would be much better if digital technology did not influence so many things.
options.D: Digital technology influences only a few specific aspects of how we live in the modern world.
options.E: The influence of digital technology is so widespread that it has completely replaced traditional life.

questions.question: Had the researchers utilized a larger sample size, their findings might have gained more widespread acceptance in the scientific community.

options.A: The scientific community accepted the findings even though the researchers used a very small sample size.
options.B: Because the researchers used such a large sample size, their findings were quickly accepted by most scientists.
options.C: The results of the study might have been more credible to other scientists if a greater number of subjects had been included.
options.D: The researchers failed to get their work accepted because they did not follow the standard scientific methods.
options.E: A larger sample size would not have made any difference in how the scientific community viewed the research.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Questions in philosophy are usually answered by combining empirical observation with logical reasoning.
options.B: Empirical observation is the only way to find answers to the deep questions posed by philosophy.
options.C: Philosophy deals with issues that cannot be resolved solely by looking at physical evidence or data.
options.D: Philosophers refuse to use empirical observation because they believe it is an unreliable source of knowledge.
options.E: The nature of philosophy is such that it never provides any definitive answers to the questions it asks.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Traditional farming practices are becoming more popular as people move from cities back to rural areas.
options.B: The growth of cities has caused a substantial reduction in the land that can be used for conventional agriculture.
options.C: There is more arable land available now than before urbanization began, thanks to new farming techniques.
options.D: Urbanization and traditional farming can coexist if the government protects certain areas of arable land.
options.E: The decrease in arable land is the main reason why so many people are moving to urban areas.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The modern system of independent nations is often said to have its origins in the Treaty of Westphalia.
options.B: The Treaty of Westphalia was the last agreement made before the modern international system was established.
options.C: Most historians disagree that the Treaty of Westphalia had any impact on the concept of sovereign states.
options.D: Sovereign states existed long before the Treaty of Westphalia, which only formalized their boundaries.
options.E: The international system would be much more stable if the Treaty of Westphalia had never been signed.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: In his new play, the author uses humor and irony to attack the wealth gaps found in today's world.
options.B: The latest play is a serious documentary about how socio-economic status affects people's lives.
options.C: The playwright's work is criticized by society because it focuses too much on economic problems.
options.D: Contemporary society is the main theme of the play, but the socio-economic disparities are ignored.
options.E: The play is a satire of the playwright's own life and his struggles with socio-economic issues.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Climate change concerns have led to a substantial growth in the utilization of renewable energy.
options.B: The increase in renewable energy use is the main cause of concerns about climate change.
options.C: Renewable energy sources are being used more because climate change is no longer a concern.
options.D: Due to the rise in renewable energy, climate change concerns have decreased significantly.
options.E: Although concerns about climate change are rising, the use of renewable energy has not increased much.

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The public began to protest the strict regulations immediately after the new law came into effect.
options.B: The new law was implemented despite the public's protests against its strict regulations.
options.C: As soon as the public protested, the new law was implemented with strict regulations.
options.D: The strict regulations of the new law were the reason why the public started protesting before it was implemented.
options.E: The public protests against the new law's strict regulations were not expected until after its implementation.

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The scientist's theories were questioned by other experts immediately after he made his results public.
options.B: Before the scientist published his results, his peers had already challenged his theories.
options.C: The scientist published his findings because his peers had challenged his theories earlier.
options.D: Peer challenges led the scientist to publish his findings as soon as possible.
options.E: The scientist's peers did not challenge his theories until long after he published his findings.

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Many people think that the discovery of fire significantly changed the course of human development.
options.B: The discovery of fire is the only reason why humans evolved the way they did.
options.C: Humans would not have evolved if fire had not been discovered at that specific time.
options.D: The turning point in human evolution was not fire, but other discoveries.
options.E: It is a known fact that human evolution began with the discovery of fire.
